---
title: "Angrist & Evans (1998) — Income Subsample Selection"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    df_print: kable
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r packages}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

source("functions_all.R")
```

---

# 1. Load Data & Build Income Sample

```{r data}
data_path  <- "/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/code/"
ae98_raw   <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "ae98.dta"))

# Base restriction (mirrors child_repl_30.Rmd exactly)
ae98 <- ae98_raw[!is.na(ae98_raw$workedm) &
                 !is.na(ae98_raw$morekids) &
                 !is.na(ae98_raw$samesex), ]

# Income variables (same as draft)
ae98$lincomem      <- log(ae98$incomem)
ae98$lincomem_cnt  <- log(100  * ae98$incomem)
ae98$lincomem_ths  <- log(ae98$incomem / 1000)
ae98$lincomem_hths <- log(ae98$incomem / 100000)

# Income sample: positive income only (same restriction as draft)
ae98_income <- ae98[!is.na(ae98$incomem) & ae98$incomem > 0, ]

cat(sprintf("Full income sample: N = %d\n", nrow(ae98_income)))
cat(sprintf("Pr(Z=1) samesex:   %.4f\n", mean(ae98_income$samesex)))
cat(sprintf("Pr(D=1) morekids:  %.4f\n", mean(ae98_income$morekids)))
```

---

# 2. Stratified Subsample (N = 3,000)

```{r sampling}
# Stratify on Z × D: 4 cells.
# Each cell keeps the same share as in the full income sample,
# so the compliance structure is preserved in the subsample.
N_sub <- 3000
set.seed(202)   # <-- record this; goes into the presentation notebook

# Step 1: compute how many to take from each cell
cell_targets <- ae98_income %>%
  mutate(cell = paste0("Z", samesex, "D", morekids)) %>%
  count(cell, name = "n_full") %>%
  mutate(n_take = pmax(1L, round(N_sub * n_full / nrow(ae98_income))))

cat("Cell targets:\n"); print(cell_targets)

# Step 2: sample each cell separately, then bind
ae98_sub <- ae98_income %>%
  mutate(cell = paste0("Z", samesex, "D", morekids)) %>%
  group_by(cell) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map2(
    cell_targets$n_take[match(
      sapply(., function(g) g$cell[1]),
      cell_targets$cell)],
    ~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(.y, nrow(.x)))
  ) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  select(-cell) %>%
  as.data.frame()

cat(sprintf("\nSubsample N = %d\n", nrow(ae98_sub)))
```

---

# 3. Comparison: Original vs. Subsample

<div style="background:#eaf4ff;border-left:4px solid #2c7bb6;padding:10px 14px;margin:12px 0;font-size:14px;">
The table below is the key check: do the subsample statistics stay close to the full income sample? If yes, results from the subsample will be representative and you can justify the choice in the thesis.
</div>

```{r comparison}
make_X_ae <- function(df) {
  model.matrix(~ agem + agefstm + sexk + sex2nd + blackm + hispm + othracem,
               data = df)
}

compare_stats <- function(df, label) {
  X <- make_X_ae(df)
  p <- logit_mle(df$samesex, X)
  kw <- kappa_weights(df$samesex, df$morekids, p)
  res <- tryCatch(
    kappa_analytic_se_all(df$lincomem, df$samesex, df$morekids, X),
    error = function(e) NULL)
  data.frame(
    Sample          = label,
    N               = nrow(df),
    `Pr(Z=1)`       = round(mean(df$samesex), 4),
    `Pr(D=1)`       = round(mean(df$morekids), 4),
    `Complier share`= round(mean(df$morekids[df$samesex==1]) -
                            mean(df$morekids[df$samesex==0]), 4),
    `mean(lincomem)`= round(mean(df$lincomem, na.rm=TRUE), 4),
    `SD(lincomem)`  = round(sd(df$lincomem,   na.rm=TRUE), 4),
    `p̂ mean`        = round(mean(p), 4),
    `p̂ SD`          = round(sd(p),   5),
    `kappa mean`    = round(mean(kw$kappa), 4),
    `tau_ml_u`      = if (!is.null(res)) round(unname(res$estimates["tau_ml_u"]), 4) else NA,
    `SE(tau_ml_u)`  = if (!is.null(res)) round(unname(res$se["tau_ml_u"]), 4) else NA,
    check.names     = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

rbind(
  compare_stats(ae98_income, "Full income sample"),
  compare_stats(ae98_sub,    "Subsample (N=3,000)")
) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Full income sample vs. stratified subsample",
      align = c("l", rep("r", 11))) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(general = paste(
    "Stratification on Z×D preserves the compliance structure."
  ))
```

---

# 4. Save Subsample

```{r save}
# Save as .rds — load in the presentation notebook with one line:
#   obj        <- readRDS(file.path(data_path, "ae98_sub.rds"))
#   ae98_sub   <- obj$ae98_sub
#   seed used  <- obj$seed

saveRDS(
  list(seed = 202, N = nrow(ae98_sub), ae98_sub = ae98_sub),
  file = file.path(data_path, "ae98_sub.rds")
)

cat(sprintf("Saved: ae98_sub.rds  (N=%d, seed=202)\n", nrow(ae98_sub)))
cat(sprintf("Reload: obj <- readRDS(file.path(data_path, 'ae98_sub.rds'))\n"))
```
